findings.title: input_guard verified at gateway level
findings.description: reflection_agent calls chat_flash() and chat_pro() from gateway/async_client.py. Both functions call sanitize_for_llm_prompt() on system_prompt AND user_prompt before sending (async_client.py:204-214 for flash, :239-249 for pro). All prediction data (prediction.prediction, original_analysis, actual_value, direction, success_value, confidence) flowing into user_prompt is sanitized for: prompt injection patterns, Markdown control chars, Unicode homoglyphs, and length truncation. No direct input_guard call needed in reflection_agent itself — the gateway is the enforcement point.
findings.remediation: None required. Gateway-level sanitization is the correct architecture per CLAUDE.md (M1 Data Integrity Protocol injected at gateway level for all LLM calls).

findings.title: LLM response JSON parsing is defensive
findings.description: _parse_reflection_response() handles three fallback paths: 1) direct json.loads, 2) regex extraction of JSON object, 3) safe default with sanitized fallback (raw_content[:200]). No unbounded string injection into downstream data structures. Root cause is validated against ROOT_CAUSE_TAXONOMY keys (line 155).
findings.remediation: None required. Defensive parsing is adequate.

findings.title: LLM-generated updated_belief stored without additional sanitization
findings.description: The 'updated_belief' field from parsed LLM response is stored verbatim in StructuredLesson (line 165) and saved to SQLite via store.save_lesson(). When later retrieved and used in expertise_discovery.generate_methodology_injection(), it passes through validate_distillation_safety() which checks for forbidden patterns. However, if a compromised/malicious LLM response injected content into updated_belief, it would be stored and potentially surfaced in future methodology injections.
findings.remediation: LOW priority. Consider adding a length cap on updated_belief (currently unbounded beyond LLM max_tokens). The entity_memory module only stores root_cause taxonomy keys in recurring_patterns, not updated_belief — so the actual injection surface through expertise_discovery is limited to root_cause values which are validated against the taxonomy.

findings.title: Batch size cap prevents resource exhaustion
findings.description: _MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 10 limits max LLM calls per batch_reflection run. Combined with MAX_PRO_CALLS_PER_SESSION=30 ceiling, this prevents runaway LLM spending from a single reflection cycle.

notes: All LLM paths are protected by gateway-level input_guard. The reflection module itself does not need to call input_guard directly — the gateway enforces sanitization on both system_prompt and user_prompt for chat_flash and chat_pro. The defensive JSON parsing with taxonomy validation prevents malformed LLM output from corrupting downstream data.
